segment.source: Renders a color-coded intensity matrix drawn by JavaScript from comma-separated column labels and 'row label | values' lines, plus a screen-reader data table with every row, column and unit. Use it for: two-dimensional value grids in a document — activity by weekday and hour, usage per team and month, risk matrices, feature adoption by segment. Prefer 'Contribution Chart' for one metric over a long time span; use 'Data Table' when exact numbers must stay visible.
Comma-separated names for the columns, left to right, e.g. Morning, Afternoon, Evening. Used for the table header and the screen-reader summary.
One row per line as "Row label | v1, v2, v3", with one value per column in column order, e.g. Mon | 42, 58, 31. Drives the accessible data table.
Optional unit appended to every value, e.g. tickets, %, or €. Shown in the table header and cells.
JSON the coloured grid reads: {"rows":["Mon","Tue"],"cols":["Morning","Afternoon"],"values":[[42,58],[55,64]]}. Keep it in sync with the rows and column labels above.
